confirmed.summary: acceptance-matrix.json is 100% placeholder after the tasks phase — the accept gate has nothing objectively checkable
confirmed.evidence: kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/acceptance-matrix.json: all 6 rows read description "Verify FR-00X is satisfied" with notes "TODO: replace with a real acceptance criterion", evidence:null, pass_fail:"pending"; proof_type is uniformly "automated_test" (wrong for FR-003/FR-004, which are documentation deliverables with no possible automated test); "negative_invariants": [] despite NFR-001 (no new SONAR_TOKEN) and NFR-002 (no suppression/ratchet/allowlist) being explicit binding invariants.
confirmed.recommendation: Replace each TODO with the real, objectively-checkable criterion the spec already provides: FR-001/002 -> automated (the SC-001a static YAML-parse assertion, plus a unit test that the extraction yields pyproject's version); FR-003/004 -> manual_review/artifact-inspection against the committed doc + investigation evidence; FR-005/006 -> automated (the offline fixture smoke + a shellcheck/no-mutation grep). Encode NFR-001 (assert no new secrets.SONAR_TOKEN reference; smoke runs with token unset) and NFR-002 (no new suppression comments) as negative_invariants.

verdict.reasoning:
Every factual claim is verified against the LIVE artifact (kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/acceptance-matrix.json): all 6 rows are "Verify FR-00X is satisfied" / TODO-marker / evidence:null / pass_fail:"pending"; proof_type is uniformly "automated_test" (6/6); negative_invariants is []. The spec has exactly FR-001..FR-006 (all placeheld) and NFR-001/NFR-002 (both absent from the matrix). Not stale: the file was committed once by finalize-tasks (commit d356831f7) and the mission is still at "planned" (pre-implementation) — this IS the current state.

I tested the two strongest refutations and they only soften the framing, not the substance. (1) By-design: the placeholder is the canonical output of scaffold_acceptance_matrix (matrix.py:215-276) invoked by finalize-tasks, "never blocks finalize" — so a pure-placeholder matrix at post-tasks is expected. (2) The accept gate (__init__.py:1063-1067) fails closed on verdict "pending", so a raw placeholder cannot silently pass. These mitigate the literal claim that the gate "has nothing objectively checkable."

But the core substance survives: validate_matrix_evidence (matrix.py:284-304) requires evidence ONLY for manual_qa, so the 6 automated_test rows can be flipped to pass_fail:"pass" with evidence:null and the gate greenlights with nothing checked (real fakeability). More decisively, negative_invariants:[] means NFR-001 (no new SONAR_TOKEN) and NFR-002 (no suppression/ratchet/allowlist) — explicit High-priority binding invariants reinforced by SC-004 — have zero machine enforcement (enforce_negative_invariants has nothing to run), which is especially pointed for a mission whose NFR-002 is "No masking of real debt." And automated_test is genuinely wrong for FR-003/FR-004, which the spec defines as committed-documentation deliverables (spec.md 41-42). The recommendation is technically sound and maps to the mission's own T002/T004 and SC-001a/SC-004 (minor imprecision: it names proof_type "manual_review" where the enum is manual_qa/code_review).

Real=true. Severity adjusted high->medium: the fakeable evidence-free rows and the unenforced binding NFRs are a genuine gate-quality/integrity gap worth fixing, but the "high / nothing objectively checkable" framing over-weights it given the fail-closed pending-verdict block and the fact that this is the expected by-design finalize scaffold at a pre-implementation phase with full runway to author real criteria before accept.

confirmed.summary: WP01 FR-002 'survives a version bump' is verified only by a static string-presence check; the runtime extraction is never executed in-mission and SC-001b is out-of-gate
confirmed.evidence: tasks/WP01-projectversion-wiring.md:44-45 (T002 is 'a pure static check' that only asserts the projectVersion wiring string is present); spec.md:68 marks SC-001b (the only runtime confirmation) as post-merge, next-nightly-cron-only, 'not a PR-gate criterion'. A broken extraction (wrong tomllib key path, or a shell command that silently emits an empty string) passes the static assertion green while shipping a non-functional fix.
confirmed.recommendation: Extract the version-derivation into a small testable step/script (e.g. scripts/ci/sonar_project_version.py reading pyproject.toml via tomllib) and add a unit test in tests/ci/ asserting it returns pyproject's version. That makes FR-002 objectively checkable within the mission instead of relying on an unobservable nightly run. Optionally add a post-merge task to trigger a workflow_dispatch and capture the real projectVersion via WP02's tool as live evidence for SC-001b.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial refutation against the live planning artifacts (mission is unimplemented: status.json event_count 0, gates post-spec/post-plan, no test file yet — so this correctly critiques the task/spec DESIGN).

All load-bearing claims verified accurate: (1) tasks/WP01-projectversion-wiring.md:45 literally defines T002 as "a pure static check, no network, no live analysis" that YAML-parses and asserts the wiring is present/sourced from pyproject.toml — it never executes the extraction. (2) spec.md:68 literally marks SC-001b as "post-merge observation," next-nightly-cron/workflow_dispatch only, "not a PR-gate criterion." (3) The sonarcloud job is gated schedule/workflow_dispatch only (never PR/push — WP01:38, plan.md:29/57, spec.md:67), so the runtime extraction genuinely never runs in-mission. (4) No WP adds an in-gate unit test of the pyproject->projectVersion derivation: WP02's version subcommand hits the live API for SC-001b post-merge, and its smoke test drives recorded API fixtures — neither tests the extraction logic; acceptance-matrix.json FR-002 is still a TODO placeholder, so no stronger proof is bound.

Refutations all fail: a static YAML parse fundamentally cannot verify runtime extraction correctness (a wrong-key or empty-emitting extraction still parses green); SC-001b only verifies out-of-gate (~24h nightly, operator-dependent, explicitly non-PR-gate); and while the trigger-gating legitimately forces LIVE ANALYSIS post-merge, it does NOT force the extraction LOGIC to be untestable — factoring it into scripts/ci/sonar_project_version.py + a tests/ci/ unit test is fully compatible with the constraint and is exactly what the repo's own Sonar Expectations ("Prefer testable extractions") and DIR-030 (producer-conformance gate) demand. The mission already uses scripts/ci/ as convention (sibling to quality_gate_decision.py), so the recommendation is cheap and idiomatic.

Minor imprecision noted but non-fatal: the "wrong tomllib key" example would actually fail loudly (KeyError) at nightly, though still out-of-gate; the "shell command silently emits empty string" example is a true silent-failure path. Core claim holds either way — in-mission gates cannot distinguish a working from a broken extraction.

Severity held at medium: not high/critical (no security/data impact; a weak post-merge SC-001b backstop exists) but not low (FR-002 is High-priority and the P1 primary deliverable #2421; the verification is genuinely fakeable inside the gate, so a silently-broken extraction ships all-green while leaving #2421 unfixed — the exact green-but-broken trap this Sonar-honesty mission exists to eliminate).

confirmed.summary: WP02 smoke test is a self-referential fixture tautology — author-recorded fixtures, an 'optional' live smoke, and no assertion tying constructed request URLs to the documented SonarCloud endpoints
confirmed.evidence: tasks/WP02-review-tool.md:50 drives the script against 'recorded JSON fixtures' and 'Optionally one live-read smoke behind a skip-if-offline guard' — so in CI the live path is skipped and the test only checks output shape against fixtures the implementer authored. Nothing verifies the script queries the correct endpoints or parses real SonarCloud responses; the fixtures can be hand-shaped to match whatever the script emits.
confirmed.recommendation: Require the fixtures to be captured from the live public API with a provenance note (URL + capture date) committed alongside them, and add an assertion that the request paths the script constructs match the documented endpoints (/api/qualitygates/project_status, /api/measures/component_tree, /api/project_analyses/search, /api/components/show, /api/issues/search). Make the live-read smoke skip only when genuinely offline (not 'optional'), so at least one path exercises real responses.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified against the live dossier at /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2421/kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/. This is a post-tasks review: no implementation exists on any ref (git log --all finds no scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh or tests/ci/test_sonarcloud_branch_review.py; status.json shows event_count 0), so the finding is critiquing the WP02 task/acceptance design, not live code.

REAL kernel (withstands refutation): The line-50 quote is accurate; the five endpoints match the documented per-subcommand endpoints (tasks/WP02-review-tool.md:44-48, FR-005). A full grep of spec.md, tasks/*, and acceptance-matrix.json confirms NOTHING requires (a) fixture provenance, (b) an assertion tying constructed request URLs to the documented endpoints, or (c) a mandatory online smoke. FR-006 (spec.md:44) explicitly permits "output shape against ... a recorded fixture" with no token, and SC-003 (spec.md:70) only requires read-only + no-token + green. So the acceptance design genuinely permits a fixture-only green test that never proves the script queries the correct endpoints. All three recommendation items target real, absent requirements — sound, low-cost hardening.

OVERSTATEMENTS (why severity drops to low, not why it is false): (1) "Self-referential tautology" ignores that the task already requires asserting arg-parsing, subcommand dispatch, AND GET-only; verifying GET-only offline forces intercepting the constructed request — the exact seam where a URL-path assertion would live — so the recommended delta is small and the required assertions are not no-ops. (2) The finding uses present-tense "the test only checks / fixtures the implementer authored" for a test that does not exist; it describes a predicted lazy implementation, not a mandated one. (3) It ignores that WP03/T005 (WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md:41) exercises the tool against the LIVE unauthenticated /api/measures/component_tree, and SC-001b confirms version/analyses against the public API post-merge — so wrong endpoints surface downstream. (4) Blast radius is low: an internal read-only diagnostic helper, not user-facing, not gating CI, firewalled from the integrity-critical NFR-002 (no debt masking).

Verdict: real but low. Genuine, verifiable gap in the smoke-test acceptance design with a sound recommendation, but the "tautology" framing and medium severity are inflated by ignoring the dispatch/GET-only assertions, the WP03 live exercise, and the tool's low blast radius.

dropped.summary: WP03's research-first investigation (FR-003, the mission's central C-002 deliverable) produces no committed evidence artifact, making the doc's core claim unfalsifiable
dropped.why_refuted:
Factual observations are accurate (WP03 owned_files = only docs/guides/coverage-signals.md; T005 "Record the evidence" has no named destination; research/ is empty and untracked, git ls-files=0; doc not yet written). But the load-bearing claim — the doc's verdict is "unfalsifiable" and "no committed artifact would contradict it," a medium drift undermining the C-002 deliverable — does not withstand scrutiny on three independent grounds.

(1) The falsification path is designed AND committed-anchored, so "unfalsifiable" is false. The internal half of the comparison is already committed (ci-quality.yml:2704 critical_paths; sonar-project.properties:4 sonar.sources=src), and the Sonar half is retrievable token-free/deterministically via GET /api/measures/component_tree — the mission's core, thrice-verified premise (spec.md:13/32/79). WP03:50 explicitly tasks the reviewer to check the doc against that live API evidence; that IS the falsification mechanism. A fabricated file-set claim would be contradicted by a trivial credential-free re-run, so "nothing would contradict it" is refuted.

(2) No artifact is structurally missing: the owned doc is the natural evidence home. The finding's own recommendation offers "an appendix in the doc" (zero change to owned_files), and T006/DoD/SC-002 already require the doc to carry the verdict "backed by evidence / backed by the FR-003 investigation." The gap is at most instruction-explicitness about inlining the raw list.

(3) Re-litigation without new evidence (DIR-003): the near-identical "unfalsifiable/subjective, any prose passes, no mechanical check" thesis was already adjudicated real=false at post-spec.json:82 (SC-002's second clause + FR-003's component_tree-vs-critical-path procedure make it an objective check against ground truth), and the "investigation is mere confirmation from committed config" angle at post-plan.json:60. This finding re-hooks the same substance onto the WP03 task file with no new evidence.

Surviving kernel is low: T005 "Record the evidence" lacks an explicit destination, and inlining the enumerated Sonar file list plus the critical-path list in a doc appendix would improve reviewability and honor DIR-003's spirit — a nice-to-have documentation-hygiene note, not a medium drift defect (the "drift" dimension is itself a mischaracterization; this is a completeness/reviewability concern). Default-to-false bar not overcome.

dropped.summary: FR-004 'discoverable + linked' DoD names no concrete link target, so 'discoverable' is not objectively checkable
dropped.why_refuted: Refuted against live artifacts. The DoD phrase "discoverable (linked from the coverage/testing context)" (WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md:45) IS objectively checkable: a reviewer greps for inbound references to the deliverable docs/guides/coverage-signals.md and confirms at least one originates from a testing/coverage/quality-gate doc (real candidates carrying such links exist: docs/guides/index.md, testing-parallel.md, testing-flakiness.md, pr-landing.md, contributing.md). The finding's own recommendation concedes "so a reviewer can grep for the reference" — the grep target is the deliverable filename, which works without pre-naming a destination. Line 50 additionally assigns "genuinely discoverable" as an explicit reviewer criterion, which the finding ignores; judgment-based discoverability is a standard, acceptable DoD form for a docs deliverable. The "e.g." hedging (which actually sits on line 42 in the T006 guidance, not lines 45-46 as the finding claims — a misattribution) is intentional and correct under C-002 research-first: the WP defers the link location until the investigation determines where a reviewer meets the coverage signal. Naming an exact target as recommended would over-constrain that research-first choice, and the suggested "testing/coverage guide index" is not even a single canonical registry (testing-parallel.md/testing-flakiness.md are NOT in how-to-toc.yml), vindicating the spec's hedge. Named targets do exist (how-to-toc.yml, how-to-index.md, testing-flakiness.md, testing-parallel.md — verified), so the factual premise is partly true, but the conclusion ("not objectively checkable") is unsupported. No boundary/producer-conformance dimension applies (FR-004 is a docs cross-link, not a code contract or two-system divergence). This is a wording-preference nit, not a real defect.

dropped.summary: issue-matrix.md lists out-of-scope issue #2416 as a row requiring a verdict, conflicting with the spec's scope
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's factual citations are accurate (issue-matrix.md:11 has an `unknown` #2416 row; spec.md:75 says #2416 already landed), but its interpretation is a category error and its actionable recommendation would violate a doctrine contract.

(1) The matrix is a deterministic machine artifact. The canonical scaffolder `src/specify_cli/tasks/issue_matrix.py` emits "one row per issue referenced in spec.md" (its verbatim header rule, lines 106-108). #2416 appears as parenthesized `(#2416)` on spec.md:75 and is regex-matched, so its row is correct-by-construction. Confirming the mechanism: the slash-joined #2419/#2420 on the same line do NOT match and correctly do NOT appear — the matrix contains exactly the 5 rows the scaffolder produces {#2421,#2422,#825,#1928,#2416}.

(2) The "conflict with scope" premise is false. The matrix is a traceability net (Gate-4/FR-037), not a work list. Scope (what the mission fixes) and traceability (what issues the spec references) are deliberately different axes. `verified-already-fixed` is an explicitly valid verdict in both the file legend and the Gate-4 doctrine (spec-kitty-mission-review/SKILL.md:587), designed precisely for already-landed work. The matrix never claims #2416 is in-scope-to-fix; it only requires it reach a verdict.

(3) The finding singles out #2416 while its logic equally condemns the explicitly-excluded epic #1928 and parent #825 — also `unknown` placeholder rows by the same rule — exposing a misunderstanding of the "one row per referenced issue" mechanism.

(4) State is correct planning-time state: status.json shows event_count 0 (no WP started); ALL rows including in-scope #2421/#2422 are `unknown` placeholders per the header ("fill at WP-implementation time"). Gate-4 only hard-fails `unknown` at review/done, so #2416 is not anomalous relative to in-scope rows.

(5) The primary recommendation ("drop the #2416 row") would directly violate the FR-037 one-row-per-referenced-issue contract; the alternative (mark it `verified-already-fixed` with the commit) is merely the designed one-line traceability fill, not busywork or evidence of a defect. The "#2421/#2422 must reach terminal verdict before done" clause just restates the guardrail Gate-4 already enforces.

The evidence is present but misread: an intentional traceability feature is mischaracterized as a scope defect. Not a real finding.

dropped.summary: Tests placed in tests/ci/ are not routed to any CI job — SC-001a/SC-003/FR-006 'runs in CI' claims are not satisfiable as written
dropped.why_refuted:
REFUTED at the stated severity/framing. The finding's mechanical premises all verify against live code: `.github/workflows/` has zero `tests/ci` references (grep exit 1); no dorny filter group globs `tests/ci/**` (core_misc at ci-quality.yml:204-235 is an explicit allow-list without it); `unit-contract-residual` selects only `-m "(unit or contract) and not (...)"` (2417-2418); `quarantine-visibility` is non-blocking (2533-2569); and root `conftest.py` (199-213) only auto-applies windows/quarantine/arch_shard, never unit/contract. So a markerless `tests/ci/*.py` truly is selected by no runnable gate.

BUT the load-bearing conclusion — that such a test "passes only locally," is "silently vacuous in CI," and the SC "runs in CI" claims are "not satisfiable as written" — is false. It ignores an always-on, purpose-built guardrail (mission #2034 / ci-suite-map-bind): `tests/architectural/test_gate_coverage.py::test_no_new_orphan_surfaces` (lines 391-424) is a hard ratchet that recollects the entire `tests/` tree via `collect_universe()` and FAILS on any test FILE selected by zero CI gates that is not in the baseline. The baseline `_gate_coverage_baseline.json` is EMPTY (`orphan_files: []`), so any new orphan reds. `CompiledGate.selects()` requires BOTH path and marker match (proven by the analyzer's own guards at 164-200 and 251-284), so a markerless test — even one whose path the `-m fast` core-misc shard collection cone covers (it does not ignore `tests/ci`) — is still classified an orphan. This ratchet is assigned to `arch_shard_2` (`tests/_arch_shard_map.py:94`), is module-marked `architectural`, and runs in the `arch-adversarial` job which is `if: always()`, group-less, with no `needs: changes` — genuinely always-on on every PR/push, and it is not `slow`-gated. Its failure message names the orphan file and instructs the author to "give the test(s) a marker a gate selects (e.g. `fast`, `integration`, `git_repo`)."

Consequently a naive markerless `tests/ci/` test cannot silently ship: it loudly reds the always-on arch pole and blocks merge, forcing the implementer to route it. SC-001a/SC-003/FR-006's "runs in CI" claims are thus structurally ENFORCED, not unsatisfiable — the exact opposite of the finding's premise. Notably the finding itself cites "the always-on arch-adversarial pole" in its recommendation yet failed to realize that pole already contains the orphan ratchet that catches any unrouted new test file anywhere in `tests/`, including `tests/ci/`.

Residual merit is at most a LOW, optional guidance nicety: the WP tasks (WP01 T002 / WP02 T004) do not proactively pre-specify a routing marker, so an implementer could write markerless-first, hit the arch red, then fix — one wasted red-first iteration, self-corrected by the existing loud gate. That is a routine TDD loop, not a coverage defect, not spec-unsatisfiability, and not "silent." Recommending the marker or the tests/architectural placement (plan.md:49 already offers the latter) is reasonable defensive advice, but the medium/coverage framing built on "silently vacuous" and "not satisfiable as written" does not withstand scrutiny. Per the refute-by-default mandate: real=false; residual value is low/informational and effectively already-handled by the repo's guardrail.

dropped.summary: WP01's 'append sonar.projectVersion in the Materialize step' option is token-gated; SC-001b observability silently depends on SONAR_TOKEN being a configured repo secret
dropped.why_refuted: Line citations are all accurate (Materialize step gated at ci-quality.yml:3106; token-check + skip at 2840-2862; the empty-token test at 2843; job-level schedule/dispatch gate at 2837; SC-001b at spec.md:68 omits SONAR_TOKEN). But the finding fails adversarial refutation as an actionable defect for four reasons. (1) The precondition it treats as a latent risk is provably satisfied: the mission's raison d'etre — a frozen 2026-03-21 new-code baseline, ~4 months of drift, and 'every analysis reports projectVersion: not provided' (spec.md:9,12) — can only exist if nightly analyses have been running, which requires SONAR_TOKEN to be configured. The missing-token scenario contradicts the observed reality the whole spec is built on, and SC-001b even confirms the reset by reading an existing analysis history via the public API. (2) The dependency is not 'silent': ci-quality.yml:2847-2861 prints a loud operator-facing banner 'SonarCloud is not configured — skipping analysis' with setup instructions into the run log, so 'would never appear [silently]' misstates the behavior. (3) SC-001b is explicitly non-gating ('Labelled post-merge; not a PR-gate criterion') and already caveats merge/push non-triggering, ~24h latency, and dispatch; the live-Sonar context is already stated in the spec's Assumptions (spec.md:79) and grounding (spec.md:13). (4) WP01 introduces no new token dependency — the token gate pre-exists and is out of scope (NFR-001 only forbids introducing one), and the finding itself concedes SC-001a, the real PR-time gate, is unaffected. Net: accurate mechanics wrapped around a non-defect that restates assumed context, mislabels a loud skip as silent, and hypothesizes a state the mission's grounding refutes. The one-line-precondition recommendation is a harmless nicety, not a fix for anything broken. Severity is informational-at-most, below a genuine low.
